reasoning:
The Effective Federal Funds Rate (EFFR) is currently 3.87% as of Nov 7, 2025 (latest data available before the Nov 9 cutoff), which reflects a recent 25bp cut by the Federal Reserve. For the target range of 3.75-4.00%, 3.87% is approximately the midpoint. In a cutting cycle, the EFFR typically remains stable near the midpoint or drifts slightly lower. 

For the first two resolution dates (Nov 16 and Dec 9), the Federal Reserve will still be operating under the current target range (the next FOMC decision is Dec 10). Historically, the EFFR can fluctuate by +/- 0.01 bps within a window, but it has spent the last five business days flat at 3.87%. The probability of a random tick up to 3.88% or higher is low (estimated at 20-25%).

For the final resolution date (Feb 7, 2026), the FOMC will have met twice (Dec and Jan). Market expectations and Reuters polls indicate a high likelihood (estimated >90%) of at least one additional rate cut by this time, which would push the EFFR significantly below 3.87%. While economic fundamentals suggest a very low probability of an increase, the forecast follows the instruction to move closer to 0.5 (increased uncertainty) for the distant horizon, reaching 0.35.
